mnemonics: ROW-MAJOR: Row by Row, Like Reading a Book
keyPoints: Multidimensional arrays are stored in contiguous memory, requiring a mapping function to translate indices to a memory address.
Row-major order stores all elements of a row consecutively, while column-major order stores all elements of a column consecutively.
The address calculation for an element `A[i][j]` in a 2D array of size `[M][N]` is: Base Address + (i * N + j) * size for row-major, and Base Address + (j * M + i) * size for column-major.
commonMistakes: Confusing row-major and column-major order, leading to incorrect address calculations.
Forgetting to multiply the final index offset by the size (in bytes) of the data type when calculating a memory address.
